hypothesis: If class-balancing strategies (such as template-frequency-weighted sampling or loss reweighting during training) are applied to retrosynthesis models trained on USPTO-retro, then top-k accuracy for rare reaction templates (frequency 1–10) will increase substantially without a corresponding decrease in accuracy for common templates, and this improvement will be most pronounced for template-based models like LocalRetro—which are most sensitive to class imbalance—while also yielding measurable gains in Pistachio OOD generalization for template-free models like RootAligned and MEGAN.
